topic: Temporal - durable execution platform for microservices orchestration

sources.title: Temporal Platform Documentation - Homepage
keyInsights: Temporal delivers crash-proof execution by guaranteeing applications resume exactly where they left off
Supports 7 official SDKs: Go, Java, Python, TypeScript, .NET, PHP, Ruby
Time-independent recovery: applications can resume seconds, days, or years after interruptions

sources.title: What is Temporal? - Core Concepts
keyInsights: Platform comprises Temporal Service (Go server + database) and Worker Processes (app code via SDKs)
Event History records workflow state at each step for recovery from last checkpoint
Workflows execute exactly once and to completion whether running for seconds or years

sources.title: How Temporal Works - Architecture Overview
keyInsights: Decoupled architecture: application code runs separately from coordinating service
Deterministic replay: workers reconstruct prior state from history without external dependencies
Tested to handle beyond 200 million executions per second

sources.title: Temporal Workflows - Overview
keyInsights: Workflows must follow deterministic constraints for consistent replay behavior
Progress tracked through Commands and Events recorded in Event History
Three key concepts: Workflow Definition, Workflow Type, Workflow Execution

sources.title: Temporal Activities - Overview
keyInsights: Activities execute single well-defined actions: API calls, DB writes, email sending
Non-deterministic code is permitted in Activities unlike Workflows
Activities should be designed to be idempotent for reliability

sources.title: Temporal Workers - Overview
keyInsights: Workers are stateless, enabling a single Worker to handle millions of open Workflow Executions
Worker Processes run external to the Temporal Service - Service never executes user code
Worker identity should be customized in cloud environments (Docker PID=1, ECS random hostnames)

sources.title: Temporal Task Queues - Comprehensive
keyInsights: Lightweight dynamically allocated queues created on demand with no explicit registration
Three types: Activity Task Queues, Workflow Task Queues, Nexus Task Queues
Default 4 partitions per queue; single partition is FIFO but limits throughput

sources.title: Temporal SDKs Overview
keyInsights: Seven official SDKs tailored to language-specific conventions
All SDKs provide Temporal Client, Workflow/Activity APIs, and Worker management
Community SDKs exist for Swift, Haskell, Clojure, and Scala

sources.title: Temporal Cloud - Overview
keyInsights: Namespace management, API key auth, mTLS certificates, SAML/SCIM integration
Audit logging, service health monitoring, capacity mode options
Cloud CLI (tcld) and Cloud Ops API for programmatic management

sources.title: Activity Failure Detection - Timeouts Deep Dive
keyInsights: Four timeout types: Schedule-To-Start, Start-To-Close, Schedule-To-Close, Heartbeat
Schedule-To-Start detects crashed workers or fleet capacity issues
Heartbeats enable progress communication and checkpointing for long-running activities

sources.title: Workflow Message Passing - Signals, Queries, Updates
keyInsights: Signals: async writes, fire-and-forget, unlimited throughput, recorded in history
Queries: sync reads, never recorded in history, efficient for state inspection
Updates: sync writes with validation, tracked results, max 10 in-flight per execution

sources.title: Child Workflows - When and How
keyInsights: Child workflows bypass parent Event History limits: 1000 children x 1000 activities = 1M activities
Parent Close Policy: TERMINATE (default), REQUEST_CANCEL, ABANDON
Rule of thumb: When in doubt, use an Activity

sources.title: Retry Policies - Complete Reference
keyInsights: Defaults: 1s initial interval, 2.0 backoff coefficient, 100s max interval, unlimited attempts
Activities retry by default; Workflows do not and should not in most cases
Prefer execution timeouts over maximum attempts to limit retry duration

sources.title: Temporal Nexus - Cross-Namespace Orchestration
keyInsights: Peer-to-peer system connecting Temporal Applications across isolated Namespaces
Services expose Operations; Endpoints decouple callers from handlers
Built-in retries, rate limiting, circuit breaking (5 consecutive errors), at-least-once semantics

sources.title: Temporal Server Configuration Defaults and Limits
keyInsights: History: 10MB warn / 50MB error; 10,240 events warn / 51,200 error
Max 2,000 pending operations (activities, children, signals); recommend < 500
gRPC: 4MB per message; Blob: 256KB warn, 2MB error

sources.title: Workflow Engine Principles - Architectural Deep Dive
keyInsights: Workflow as Code: standard code with automatic state preservation across failures
Transfer Queues implement transactional outbox pattern for atomic consistency
System scales through multiple workflows, hosts, and partitioned databases - not individual workflow scaling

sources.title: Temporal Server GitHub Repository
keyInsights: Written in Go (99.5% of codebase), MIT license, 19.5k stars
Originated as fork of Uber's Cadence
Supports PostgreSQL and Cassandra database backends

sources.title: TypeScript Hello World Workflow Sample
keyInsights: proxyActivities<typeof activities>() provides type-safe activity stubs
Workflows are async functions that await activity results
Import only types from activities to maintain sandbox isolation

sources.title: Python Hello World Activity Sample
keyInsights: @workflow.defn class with @workflow.run async method pattern
workflow.execute_activity() with typed parameters and timeout
Worker registers workflows list and activities list with task queue

sources.title: Go Hello World Workflow Sample
keyInsights: workflow.ExecuteActivity(ctx, Activity, name).Get(ctx, &result) pattern
Activities use standard context.Context; Workflows use workflow.Context
activity.GetLogger(ctx) for structured logging within activities

sources.title: Java Hello World Activity Sample
keyInsights: @WorkflowInterface and @ActivityInterface with @WorkflowMethod and @ActivityMethod
Workflow.newActivityStub() for typed activity proxies with ActivityOptions
Interface-based design with separate implementation classes

sources.title: TypeScript Signals, Queries, Updates - Complete Code
keyInsights: defineSignal/defineQuery/defineUpdate with type parameters for type safety
setHandler with optional validator for Updates
Mutex from async-mutex for concurrent handler safety

sources.title: Go Signals, Queries, Updates - Code Patterns
keyInsights: Signal handling via workflow.GetSignalChannel(ctx, name).Receive()
SetQueryHandler for synchronous state inspection
SetUpdateHandlerWithOptions with Validator for validated mutations

sources.title: Python Signals, Queries, Updates - Decorator Patterns
keyInsights: @workflow.signal, @workflow.query, @workflow.update decorators on class methods
@update_name.validator decorator for validation logic
Signal-With-Start pattern for atomic start-and-signal operations

sources.title: Java Signals, Queries, Updates - Annotation Patterns
keyInsights: @SignalMethod, @QueryMethod, @UpdateMethod, @UpdateValidatorMethod annotations
Direct method call semantics on typed workflow stubs
Validator linked to update via updateName parameter

sources.title: TypeScript Versioning - Patching and Worker Versioning
keyInsights: Three-step patching: patched() -> deprecatePatch() -> remove
Worker Versioning as alternative: version-pinned task queue partitions
Replay Testing essential for verifying patch safety in CI/CD

sources.title: TypeScript Testing Guide
keyInsights: Integration tests recommended as majority of test suite
TestWorkflowEnvironment.createTimeSkipping() for fast Workflow testing
Worker.runReplayHistory() for determinism validation in CI/CD

sources.title: Go Testing Guide
keyInsights: Testify-based WorkflowTestSuite with fresh TestWorkflowEnvironment per test
Mock Activity signatures validated against originals at test time
WorkflowReplayer for history-based determinism verification

sources.title: Building Reliable Distributed Systems in Node - Blog
keyInsights: Food delivery example: transient vs non-transient error differentiation
Compensating transactions (refunds) built into saga pattern naturally
await condition(() => state, timeout) replaces database polling

sources.title: TypeScript Timers - Durable Sleep Patterns
keyInsights: Timers are durable: persist across Worker and service restarts
Resource-light: millions of timers from a single Worker
Promise.race for dynamic timeout patterns; condition() for updatable timers

sources.title: TypeScript Continue-As-New - Implementation
keyInsights: wf.continueAsNew<typeof workflow>() with state parameter
Check workflowInfo().continueAsNewSuggested before continuing
Never call from Signal or Update handlers - wait for completion first

sources.title: TypeScript Child Workflows - Code Examples
keyInsights: startChild() returns handle for signal/query without awaiting completion
executeChild() awaits child completion before proceeding
getExternalWorkflowHandle() for cross-workflow interaction

sources.title: Go Continue-As-New Implementation
keyInsights: workflow.NewContinueAsNewError(ctx, Workflow, input) terminates and creates new execution
GetInfo(ctx).GetContinueAsNewSuggested() for automatic threshold detection
State passed as struct parameter to next execution

sources.title: Temporal Visibility - Search and Filtering
keyInsights: SQL-like query syntax for listing, filtering, sorting Workflow Executions
Custom search attributes: strings, ints, booleans, datetimes, keyword lists
MySQL 8.0.17+, PostgreSQL 12+, Elasticsearch 7+, OpenSearch 2+

sources.title: Temporal Cloud Pricing
keyInsights: Essentials $100/mo (1M actions), Business $500/mo (2.5M), Enterprise contact sales (10M)
Volume discounts from $50/M down to $25/M at 200M+ actions
$1,000 free credits for new users; $6,000 Startup Program

sources.title: Temporal Cluster Metrics Reference
keyInsights: Key metrics: service_requests, service_latency, task_requests, poll_success/timeouts
persistence_errors and persistence_latency for database health monitoring
Grafana dashboards available in community repository

sources.title: TypeScript Observability Guide
keyInsights: Replay-safe Workflow loggers prevent duplicate messages during replay
Prometheus and OpenTelemetry metric emission via Worker configuration
Sinks for one-way data export from Workflow sandbox isolates

sources.title: Go Observability Guide
keyInsights: OpenTelemetry, OpenTracing, and Datadog interceptors for distributed tracing
Tally library integration for custom metrics reporting
UpsertSearchAttributes for runtime visibility attribute updates

sources.title: TypeScript Cancellation Patterns
keyInsights: Activity cancellation requires heartbeating with Heartbeat Timeout
Cleanup logic in finally clause; must re-throw for canceled status
Local Activities can be canceled without heartbeats

sources.title: TypeScript Failure Detection
keyInsights: ApplicationFailure.create() for explicit workflow/activity failures
Non-retryable flag prevents retry for permanent failures
Three workflow timeout types: Execution, Run, and Task

sources.title: Temporal Use Cases - Industries and Patterns
keyInsights: Vinted: 10-12M workflows/day for payments; VEED.IO: 4M daily video workflows
Replit uses Temporal for AI agent orchestration at massive scale
Use cases span payments, AI agents, video processing, infrastructure, subscriptions

sources.title: Temporal for AI - Agent Orchestration
keyInsights: Durable execution for LLM calls: automatic retries for rate limiting and timeouts
Human-in-the-loop via Signals and Updates for agent approval gates
2x faster AI feature shipping claimed; used by Replit, Retool, Gorgias

sources.title: Temporal Event Types Reference
keyInsights: Events created by Temporal Service in response to external occurrences and Commands
Categories: Workflow, Activity, Timer, Signal, Child Workflow, Update, Nexus
Each category has Scheduled, Started, Completed, Failed, TimedOut, Canceled events

sources.title: Temporal Server Dynamic Configuration
keyInsights: Frontend: 2,400 RPS/host; History: 3,000 RPS/host, 9,000 persistence QPS
Matching: 1,200 RPS/host, 4 default partitions
All values tunable per namespace or task queue via constraint specifications

sources.title: Temporal Glossary - Key Definitions
keyInsights: Workflow Execution: durable, scalable, reliable, reactive function execution
Event History: append-only log representing full state of a Workflow Execution
Namespace: unit of isolation within the Temporal Platform

sources.title: Deploying Workers to ECS - Production Guide
keyInsights: Fargate Spot for 50-70% cost savings; stateless workers handle retries naturally
CPU target ~25% for scale-out before bottleneck; asymmetric cooldowns
No load balancer needed; health via lightweight HTTP endpoint

sources.title: TypeScript Schedules - Recurring Workflows
keyInsights: Schedules replace cron workflows with better management and overlap control
Interval, calendar, and one-time specifications supported
Backfill for missed executions; pause/resume without affecting running workflows

sources.title: Temporal Security Overview
keyInsights: mTLS required for Temporal Cloud; end-to-end encryption available
Security whitepaper: provable security by design
Self-hosted and Cloud security guides available separately

sources.title: Temporal CLI Reference
keyInsights: temporal server start-dev: local dev with Web UI, no dependencies
Commands: workflow, activity, schedule, task-queue, operator, batch, server
Environment variables and named environments for multi-target configuration

sources.title: Temporal Cloud Service Availability
keyInsights: p99 latency SLO of 200ms per region
Actual Jan 2026: StartWorkflow p99=69ms, Signal p99=46ms
On-Demand and Provisioned capacity modes available

sources.title: Platform Control Plane on Temporal - Blog
keyInsights: Infrastructure provisioning: durable workflows eliminate orphaned resources from script failures
Entity Workflow patterns for long-running infrastructure lifecycle management
Netflix uses Temporal for infrastructure control plane at enterprise scale

sources.title: Temporal 101 TypeScript Course
keyInsights: Free 2-hour self-paced course covering Workflows and Activities basics
Covers failure recovery, execution model, event history, Web UI, CLI
Available in TypeScript, Go, Java, and Python

sources.title: Temporal Blog - Latest Posts
keyInsights: Series D: $300M at $5B valuation, revenue +380% YoY, usage +350%
Worker Versioning GA and Upgrade on Continue-As-New (March 2026)
WorkerResourceTemplate for auto-scaling versioned Workers on Kubernetes
